records.ety: [L. exorcizare, Gr. 'exorki`zein; 'ex out + "orki`zein to make one swear, bind by an oath, fr. "o`rkos oath: cf. F. exorciser.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Exorcised (-sīzd); p. pr. & vb. n. Exorcising (-sī`zng).]

defs: To cast out, as a devil, evil spirits, etc., by conjuration or summoning by a holy name, or by certain ceremonies; to expel (a demon) or to conjure (a demon) to depart out of a person possessed by one.
quotes: He impudently excorciseth devils in the church.

defs: To deliver or purify from the influence of an evil spirit or demon.
quotes: Exorcise the beds and cross the walls.
Mr. Spectator . . . do all you can to exorcise crowds who are . . . processed as I am.
